title: Measures of Central Tendency
description: Compute arithmetic mean, median, mode, geometric mean, and harmonic mean.
explanation: The program reads data as a vector and computes different measures of central tendency and displays them with a bar chart.

example.output: Displays mean, median, mode, GM, HM

title: Measures of Dispersion
description: Compute range, quartile deviation, mean deviation, standard deviation, and variance.
explanation: This program evaluates different measures that describe spread of data.

title: Angle Between Two Surfaces
description: Compute angle between two surfaces at a point using gradients.
explanation: Gradients of both surfaces at a point give normal vectors. The angle between normals gives angle between surfaces.

title: Linear Curve Fitting y = ax + b
description: Fit a straight line to experimental data using least squares.
explanation: Uses normal equations to compute slope and intercept.

title: Power Curve Fitting y = ax^b
description: Fit power law curve using logarithmic transformation.
explanation: Log transform converts nonlinear relation into linear form for least squares fitting.

title: Quadratic Curve Fitting
description: Fit quadratic curve y = ax^2 + bx + c using least squares.
explanation: Uses polynomial regression to compute best fit quadratic equation.
